title: music_reproduction
summary: Reproduce the reference music piece into a correct multitrack output that matches its rhythm, tempo, key, and time signature.
taskPrompt:
Goal: Reproduce the accompaniment/backing track of a piece of music as a DAW project from audio alone. Listen to the reference audio, identify all instrumental parts (ignore vocals and other non-reproducible elements), tempo, key, and time signature, then recreate the instrumental accompaniment using any DAW available on the system (e.g., Cubase, Logic Pro, FL Studio, Ableton Live) with appropriate virtual instruments.

Input:
- piano_with_organ\input\reference_audio.wav — the reference audio recording (stereo). This is the ONLY input. No metadata or task brief is provided. You must infer tempo, key, time signature, and instrumentation from the audio. Focus on reproducing the instrumental/accompaniment parts only — skip vocals.

Steps:
1. Listen to the reference audio to identify all instrumental parts, tempo, key, and time signature. Ignore vocals and any non-reproducible elements.
2. Open any DAW available on the system and create a new empty project.
3. Set the project tempo and time signature based on what you hear.
4. Create one MIDI/Instrument track per identified instrument, naming each track after the instrument.
5. Assign an appropriate virtual instrument to each track. The patch does not need to be identical to the original, but must belong to the correct instrument family.
6. Transcribe all notes, rhythms, and dynamics for each instrument by entering MIDI data.
7. Adjust velocity and expression to reflect the dynamic contour of each part.
8. Export outputs:
   a. Export MIDI file > save as piano_with_organ\output\transcription.mid
   b. Export audio mixdown (WAV stereo) > save as piano_with_organ\output\mixdown.wav
   c. For EACH track: solo the track, export audio mixdown > save as piano_with_organ\output\stems\<track_name>.wav
   d. Save the DAW project file in the project's own location.
9. Take a screenshot of the DAW arrange/timeline view showing all tracks with MIDI regions visible:
   save_milestone_screenshot(path="piano_with_organ\output\overview.png", description="DAW arrange view with all tracks")

Output files (all saved to piano_with_organ\output):
- transcription.mid: Exported MIDI file with GM program assignments per track.
- mixdown.wav: Stereo audio mixdown.
- stems/: Per-track solo WAV exports (one file per instrument track).
- overview.png: Screenshot of the DAW arrange/timeline view.

Verification:
- Gate 1: transcription.mid must contain at least 1 note.
- Gate 2: mixdown.wav must be non-silent.
- Gate 3: stems/ must contain at least 1 non-silent WAV.
- Gate 4: overview.png must show the DAW arrange/timeline view.
- Check 1 (25%): Pitch accuracy — MIDI notes vs reference (F1 on pitch + onset).
- Check 2 (25%): Rhythm accuracy — MIDI durations vs reference (F1 on onset + duration).
- Check 3 (10%): Dynamics — loudness contour correlation of per-track stems.
- Check 4 (10%): Instrument assignment — GM program number accuracy.
- Check 5 (20%): Timbre consistency — audio similarity of per-track stems.
- Check 6 (10%): Arrangement quality — track organization and naming.
agentMustDo: Listen to `reference_audio.wav` from the input directory to identify all instrumental parts (ignoring vocals and non-reproducible elements), tempo, key signature, and time signature.
Open any DAW available on the system (e.g., Cubase, Logic Pro, FL Studio, Ableton Live) and create a new empty project.
Set the project tempo and time signature based on what was heard in the audio.
Create one MIDI/Instrument track per identified instrument, naming each track after the instrument.

Assign an appropriate virtual instrument to each track. The chosen patch does not need to be identical to the original recording, but must belong to the correct instrument family (e.g., a string patch for violin, a brass patch for trumpet).
Transcribe all notes, rhythms, and dynamics for each instrument track by entering MIDI data.
Adjust velocity and expression (CC) to reflect the dynamic contour of each part.
**Mixdown**: Export audio mixdown (WAV stereo) > save as `output/mixdown.wav`
**Stems**: For each track, solo the track, export audio mixdown > save as `output/stems/<track_name>.wav`
Take a screenshot of the DAW arrange/timeline view showing all tracks with MIDI regions visible > save as `output/overview.png`

inputFiles.name: Reference recording
inputFiles.description: Stereo audio of the piece to reproduce. This is the **only input** the agent receives — no metadata, no task brief.

referenceFiles.name: MIDI export
referenceFiles.description: Exported MIDI with GM program assignments per track

referenceFiles.name: Audio mixdown
referenceFiles.description: Stereo mixdown (gate: must exist, >= 50 KB, non-silent)

referenceFiles.name: Per-track stems
referenceFiles.description: One WAV per track, solo-exported (gate: >= 1 non-silent file)

referenceFiles.name: Screenshot
referenceFiles.description: DAW arrange/timeline view with MIDI tracks visible

evaluation:
### MIDI Preprocessing

Before any MIDI-level comparison, both agent and reference MIDI are preprocessed. The preprocessing is **instrument-aware**, with different rules for keyboard instruments (GM program 0–15: Piano + Chromatic Percussion) vs. other instruments:

| | Keyboard (GM 0–15) | Other instruments |
|---|---|---|
| Keyswitch filtering | **Not applied** — these instruments legitimately use low pitches (e.g., piano C0, E0) | Discard all notes with pitch < 24 (C1) to remove keyswitch trigger notes |
| CC64 (sustain pedal) normalization | **Applied** — note durations are extended to their effective sounding duration when CC64 is active at note-off. Extension continues until CC64 releases (value < 64) or the next note-on of the same pitch, whichever comes first. | **Not applied** — sustain pedal is rarely used and does not affect sounding duration in the same way |

This ensures that two musically equivalent representations — e.g., short notes + sustain pedal vs. long notes without pedal — produce the same effective durations for rhythm F1 comparison.

### Hard gate conditions (auto score=0 if triggered):

- Gate 1: `transcription.mid` missing or contains 0 notes after preprocessing -> score = 0
- Gate 2: `mixdown.wav` missing, < 50 KB, or silent (RMS <= -60 dB) -> score = 0
- Gate 3: `output/stems/` missing or contains 0 non-silent WAV files -> score = 0
- Gate 4: `overview.png` missing or does not show DAW arrange/timeline view with MIDI tracks -> score = 0 (LLM vision judge, YES/NO)

### Scoring method (all gates pass -> weighted sum up to 1.0):

Evaluation is **stem-driven**: only the k instruments that have reference stems are scored (k = number of reference stem files). The reference MIDI may contain more tracks than available stems (e.g., the full MIDI has 20 tracks but only 8 stems are provided); tracks without reference stems are logged but ignored in scoring.

Evaluation is split into two layers: **MIDI layer** for composition accuracy (pitch, rhythm, instrument assignment — these are VST-agnostic) and **audio layer** for dynamics and timbre (these depend on how the chosen virtual instrument actually sounds, so raw MIDI CC/velocity comparison would be unreliable across different sound libraries).

| Check | Weight | Method | Details |
|---|---|---|---|
| Pitch accuracy | 0.25 | MIDI F1 | F1 on `(pitch, quantized_onset)` tuples (after preprocessing). Hungarian matching pairs agent tracks to reference tracks. Quantization resolution = `min(shortest_note_in_ref, sixteenth_note_at_BPM)`. |
| Rhythm accuracy | 0.25 | MIDI F1 | F1 on `(quantized_onset, quantized_duration)` tuples across matched track pairs (after preprocessing — keyboard instruments use CC64-normalized durations). |
| Dynamics | 0.10 | Audio loudness contour correlation | Per matched track pair: extract RMS/LUFS loudness contour from agent stem and reference stem, compute Pearson correlation. Score = mean correlation across all matched tracks. |
| Instrument assignment | 0.10 | Exact match | Percentage of matched tracks with correct GM program number vs the reference MIDI. Score = correct / total. |
| Timbre consistency | 0.20 | Audio embedding cosine similarity | Per matched track pair: extract MFCC embeddings from agent stem and reference stem. Compute cosine similarity. Score = mean similarity across all matched tracks, thresholded: >= 0.80 -> full credit, linearly scaled below. Implicitly captures articulation/technique correctness. |
| Arrangement quality | 0.10 | LLM vision judge | Screenshot assessed for: correct track count, logical track naming/ordering, professional project organization. YES (1.0) / NO (0.0). |

**What does a PERFECT output look like?**
- All notes, rhythms faithfully match the reference MIDI -> pitch F1 ~ 1.0, rhythm F1 ~ 1.0
- Loudness contours of each stem closely follow the reference stems -> dynamics ~ 1.0
- GM program numbers correctly assigned -> instrument assignment = 1.0
- Chosen virtual instruments sound like the correct instrument families, with correct articulations -> timbre cosine similarity >= 0.80
- Clean DAW project with properly named tracks -> arrangement = 1.0
- **Total ~ 1.0**

**What does a WRONG/empty output look like?**
- Missing `.mid`, `.wav`, or stems -> gate failure -> score = 0.0
- Random notes or empty MIDI -> pitch/rhythm F1 ~ 0.0
- Piano patch used for every instrument -> timbre similarity low, instrument assignment = 0.0
- Flat dynamics throughout -> dynamics correlation ~ 0.0
- **Total < 0.1**

evaluatorCredentials.purpose: OpenAI vision/LLM judge scores the deliverable during evaluate(); sourced from secret/eval_time/openai.env

classifier.rationale: The task involves reproducing music using digital audio workstations, fitting within audio and music production.
